title: Top 9 Herbal Essential Oils for Instant Relief from Hot Flashes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valeranone is good for hot flashes because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce the production of free radicals, which can cause hot flashes by affecting the body's hormone balance.

Valerenic acid is good for hot flashes because it has a calming effect on the body, which helps to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes by regulating hormonal imbalances and promoting relaxation.
Valerenal is good for hot flashes because it has a calming effect on the body, helping to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes by regulating hormones and promoting relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oil, also known as valerian oil, is a natural remedy that may help with hot flashes. This oil is extracted from the root of the valerian plant, which has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. The oil contains a compound called valerenic acid, which has a soothing effect on the nervous system. When hot flashes occur, the body's temperature regulation is disrupted, causing sudden and intense heat. Valerian oil's calming properties may help to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes by regulating body temperature and promoting relaxation. Additionally, valerian oil has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may also contribute to its potential benefits for hot flashes. When inhaled or applied topically, the oil's active compounds may help to calm the body and mind, reducing the symptoms associated with hot flashes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with hot flashes, you'll need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a solvent oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil. Start by measuring out 1 cup of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and placing them in a clean glass jar. Next, pour 2 cups of solvent oil over the leaves, making sure they're completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil extract from the leaves. After the waiting period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil can be used topically, but be sure to dilute it with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil before applying it to the skin. Use a few drops of the oil in a diffuser or inhale it directly to help alleviate hot flashes, but remember to consult with a healthcare professional before using any new herbal remedy.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for hot flashes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Actein is good for hot flashes because it may help balance hormone levels in the body, which in turn can reduce the frequency and severity of hot flash episodes.
Isoferulic acid is good for hot flashes because it may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to the frequency and severity of hot flashes.

Phenolic acids are good for hot flashes because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help regulate body temperature and alleviate symptoms of hot flashes.

Isorhapontigenin is good for hot flashes because it helps to balance the body's hormones and reduce the symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, by acting as an antioxidant that protects the body from cell damage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa, also known as black cohosh, is a plant-based essential oil that has been used for centuries to help alleviate hot flashes. This oil contains compounds that may help to calm the nervous system and reduce the production of estrogen, which is often associated with hot flashes. When estrogen levels drop, it can cause hot flashes, night sweats, and mood swings. The active ingredients in Cimicifuga racemosa, such as actein and triterpene glycosides, may help to block the effects of estrogen decline, reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes. Additionally, Cimicifuga racemosa has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body, further reducing hot flash symptoms. Studies have shown that women who use Cimicifuga racemosa essential oil experience a significant reduction in hot flashes, making it a popular alternative remedy for menopause symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with hot flashes, you need fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. First, dry the leaves completely in a warm, shaded area or using a food dehydrator. Then, place the dried leaves in a clean glass jar with a lid, leaving about an inch at the top for the leaves to expand during the infusion process. Next, cover the jar with a paper towel or a coffee filter and place it in a saucepan with water, making sure the water level is below the bottom of the jar. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for 2-3 hours. After the infusion, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, let the liquid sit at room temperature for a few days to allow the water to evaporate and the essential oil to separate from the liquid. Once the oil has risen to the top, carefully pour it into a small glass bottle and seal it tightly. This Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil can be used as a natural remedy to help alleviate hot flashes.
remedies.image_alt: Cimicifuga racemosa herbal essential oils for hot flashes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for hot flashes because it helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, which can lower body temperature and alleviate the symptoms of hot flashes.
Shogaol is good for hot flashes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the nervous system and reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes.
Zingiberene is good for hot flashes because it helps to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which can reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oil, also known as ginger oil, is often used to help reduce hot flashes. This is because ginger oil has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the body. When a woman experiences a hot flash, her body is sending out signals to the brain, telling it to release stress hormones like adrenaline and cortisol. These hormones can cause blood vessels to dilate, which leads to the feeling of heat. Ginger oil has been shown to help slow down the release of these stress hormones, which can help to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes. Additionally, ginger oil has been found to have a balancing effect on the body's temperature regulation, helping to keep the body cool and calm even in hot conditions. This can be especially helpful for women who experience hot flashes due to hormonal imbalances during menopause.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with hot flashes, you'll need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil, and a clean glass jar with a lid. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves completely in a warm, dark place, then crush them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle. Next, combine 1 part of the crushed leaves with 5 parts of the carrier oil in the glass jar, and seal it tightly. Store the jar in a warm, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every few days, to allow the oil to infuse with the herb's properties. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil is now ready to use. To apply it, mix a few drops with a carrier oil and massage it onto your skin, or add a few drops to your bath water for relaxation and relief from hot flashes.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for hot flashes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for hot flashes because it has a cooling effect on the body and helps to reduce the frequency and severity of these flashes by balancing body temperature and hormone levels.
Linalyl acetate is good for hot flashes because it has a calming effect on the body, which helps to regulate the body's temperature and reduce symptoms of hot flashes.
Caryophyllene may help reduce hot flashes by acting as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can help lower body temperature and reduce the severity of symptoms.

R Osmanic acid is good for hot flashes because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help calm the body's nervous system and reduce blood vessel dilation, which are the main causes of hot flashes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oil, also known as lavender oil, is often used to help with hot flashes. This is because it has a cooling effect on the body. When applied to the skin or inhaled, it can help lower body temperature and reduce the sensation of heat. The oil also contains a chemical called linalool, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. This can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for hot flashes. Additionally, lavender oil has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax muscles and improve blood flow. This can help alleviate hot flashes caused by hormonal imbalances and other factors. The oil's ability to regulate body temperature and reduce stress makes it a popular natural remedy for managing hot flashes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with hot flashes, start by harvesting fresh Ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted and organic source. Choose the leaves with the most vibrant green color, as they are richer in medicinal properties. Next, dry the leaves thoroughly by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper towel or a clean cloth, allowing them to air dry for 24 to 48 hours. Once the leaves are completely dry, use a coffee grinder or a herb grinder to crush them into a fine powder. Then, combine the powdered Ginkgo biloba leaves with a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1 part Ginkgo biloba to 3 parts carrier oil. Use a double boiler or a dedicated oil infuser to heat the mixture gently, usually around 100 to 150 degrees Fahrenheit, for 2 to 3 hours to extract the medicinal properties from the Ginkgo biloba leaves. After the extraction process, strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Store the Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil in a cool, dark place and use it as directed to help alleviate hot flashes.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for hot flashes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Ligustilide may be good for hot flashes because it helps reduce inflammation and relax blood vessels, which can lower body temperature and reduce the frequency of hot flashes.

Phenolic acids are good for hot flashes because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, which in turn can reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes in women.

Coumarins may be good for hot flashes because they help improve blood flow to the brain, which can reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes by balancing hormone levels and promoting relaxation.
Butylphthalide is good for hot flashes because it helps to improve blood flow and relax blood vessels, which can reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes in women.

Lignans are good for hot flashes because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help calm the nervous system and regulate body temperature, which in turn can reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica essential oil is a natural remedy that may help ease hot flashes. This oil is extracted from the roots and stems of the angelica plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The oil contains compounds like terpenes and sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help calm the nervous system and reduce the production of stress hormones that can trigger hot flashes. Additionally, angelica archangelica essential oil has been shown to have a cooling effect on the body, which may help lower body temperature and alleviate hot flash symptoms. Some studies suggest that the oil may also have a balancing effect on hormones, which could help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce hot flashes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with hot flashes, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. Start by measuring 1 cup of dried leaves and place them in a clean glass jar. Next, combine 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and 1 cup of vodka in a separate container. Pour the oil and vodka mixture over the dried leaves in the jar, making sure that the leaves are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. You can now use the Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil as needed by mixing a few drops with a carrier oil and applying it to the skin, or by inhaling it directly from a cloth or handkerchief. However, it is essential to note that Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals, so perform a patch test before using it extensively.

Quercetin is good for hot flashes because it helps to calm the body's nervous system and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to the frequency and severity of hot flashes.

Tannins are good for hot flashes because they have a cooling effect on the body, helping to reduce the severity and frequency of hot flashes by increasing blood flow and improving the body's ability to regulate its temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oil, also known as oats oil, is a natural remedy for hot flashes. This oil is extracted from the oat plant and has been used for centuries to soothe and calm the body. Avena sativa oil is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger hot flashes. The oil works by regulating hormones and balancing the body's temperature, making it a popular choice for women experiencing menopause symptoms. Avena sativa oil is also rich in antioxidants, which help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals and promote overall well-being. Studies have shown that using Avena sativa oil topically or taking it internally can help to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes. The oil is also non-habit forming and free from harsh chemicals, making it a safe and natural alternative to traditional treatments for hot flashes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with hot flashes, you will need to start by gathering the necessary materials. First, dry Ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers thoroughly in a warm, well-ventilated area, away from direct sunlight. Once dry, use a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle to finely crush the leaves and flowers. Next, mix 1 cup of the crushed Ginkgo biloba with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days, allowing the oil to extract the beneficial compounds from the Ginkgo biloba. After the extraction period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil is now ready to use, but it's essential to note that it should be diluted with a carrier oil before applying it to the skin, as it can be quite potent.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal essential oils for hot flashes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for hot flashes because they have antioxidant properties that help balance hormones and improve blood vessel health, reducing the frequency and severity of hot flashes in women.

Agnuside is good for hot flashes because it can help lower body temperature and reduce symptoms of hot flashes by acting as a natural vasodilator, which means it can help dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow to the skin.

Iridoid glycosides are good for hot flashes because they help to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes by improving blood circulation and regulating hormonal balance, thus providing relief from the uncomfortable symptoms associated with menopause.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus essential oil is a natural remedy that may help reduce hot flashes in menopausal women. This oil is made from the seeds of the chaste tree, which has been used for centuries to balance hormones and alleviate symptoms of menopause. The oil's active compounds, such as aucubin and agnuside, may help regulate the body's hormone levels, particularly estrogen and progesterone, which can become imbalanced during menopause and cause hot flashes. By balancing hormone levels, Vitex agnus-castus essential oil may help reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes, making it a popular natural remedy for women experiencing this symptom. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe and calm the body, reducing the discomfort and anxiety associated with hot flashes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with hot flashes, you'll need some basic supplies and a bit of patience. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves thoroughly by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper bag or a clean cloth, away from direct sunlight and heat. Next, chop the dried leaves into small pieces and add them to a clean glass jar, filling it about 2/3 full. Pour a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil over the leaves, making sure they're completely covered. Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark spot for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar gently every few days to help the oil extract from the leaves. After the infusion time has passed, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Finally, store your Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil in a cool, dark place and use it as needed to help alleviate hot flashes, but be sure to do a patch test first to ensure you don't have any sensitivity to the oil.
remedies.image_alt: Vitex agnus-castus herbal essential oils for hot flashes

Luteolin is good for hot flashes because it helps reduce inflammation and calm the nervous system, which in turn can reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes in some women.
Apigenin is good for hot flashes because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to regulate body temperature and reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes.
Quercetin is good for hot flashes because it helps to calm the nervous system and reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can cause sudden and intense heat sensations.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oil, also known as lemon balm oil, has been used to help with hot flashes. This is because it has a calming effect on the body. When you're feeling stressed or anxious, your body temperature can rise, leading to hot flashes. Melissa officinalis oil helps to calm your nervous system, reducing the stress and anxiety that can cause hot flashes. It also has a cooling effect on the body, which can help to bring down your body temperature and reduce the discomfort of a hot flash. Additionally, Melissa officinalis oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the inflammation that can be associated with hot flashes. By reducing stress, cooling the body, and reducing inflammation, Melissa officinalis essential oil may be able to help alleviate hot flashes and make you feel more comfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that helps with hot flashes, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil. First, make sure your Ginkgo biloba leaves are free of any contaminants or mold. You can dry them yourself by placing them in a single layer on a paper towel and letting them air dry for a few days. Next, combine 1 cup of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the oil infuse into the leaves. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. The resulting Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil can be stored in a cool, dark place and used as a natural remedy to help alleviate hot flashes. It's recommended to start with a small amount and gradually increase the dose as needed, and to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you are pregnant or have any underlying medical conditions.

Isorhapontigenin is good for hot flashes because it helps to reduce the production of hormones that cause hot flashes in the body by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, which in turn helps to calm down the body's overactive response to these hormones.

Bilobalide may be good for hot flashes because it helps to regulate blood flow to the brain and body, which can reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes by improving hormone balance and reducing stress on the body's temperature control system.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oil is believed to help reduce hot flashes. The oil contains flavonoids, which are plant chemicals that can improve blood flow to the brain and other organs. This increase in blood flow may help regulate body temperature, reducing the occurrence of hot flashes. Ginkgo biloba also contains terpenoids, which are known to have anti-inflammatory properties. By reducing inflammation, the oil may help alleviate other symptoms associated with hot flashes, such as headaches and mood swings. The flavonoids in ginkgo biloba may also help to balance hormone levels, which can contribute to hot flashes in some women.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil that may help with hot flashes, you will need some fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil, and a clean glass jar with a lid. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves completely if they are fresh, either by air-drying them or using a food dehydrator on the lowest temperature setting. Then, chop the dried leaves into small pieces and add them to the glass jar. Next, pour the carrier oil over the Ginkgo biloba leaves, making sure that they are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called infusion, and it allows the active compounds in the Ginkgo biloba leaves to be extracted into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oil can be used topically, applied to the skin with a few drops at a time, or added to a hot bath for potential relief from hot flashes.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for hot flashes

intro_desc: Hot flashes are a common issue for many women, especially during menopause. They can be uncomfortable, embarrassing, and disrupt daily life. Herbal essential oils can be a natural and effective way to manage hot flashes. Two herbs that have shown promise in reducing hot flashes are Valeriana officinalis and Cimicifuga racemosa.  Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, has a calming effect on the body. It helps to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for hot flashes. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress hormones, valerian root can help to minimize the frequency and severity of hot flashes. Additionally, valerian root has been shown to regulate body temperature, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of hot flashes.  Cimicifuga racemosa, also known as black cohosh, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce hot flashes. It works by regulating estrogen levels and reducing inflammation in the body. This can help to alleviate the symptoms of hot flashes, such as sweating, flushing, and rapid heartbeat.  Fixing the issue of hot flashes is important because it can significantly improve a woman's quality of life. Hot flashes can disrupt sleep, cause anxiety and stress, and affect daily activities. By managing hot flashes, women can feel more confident and in control of their lives. They can enjoy activities they love without the fear of having a hot flash, and they can maintain their independence and mobility.  Using herbal essential oils can be a safe and natural way to manage hot flashes. They can be inhaled directly, added to bath water, or used in aromatherapy. By incorporating valerian root and black cohosh into your daily routine, you can reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes and improve your overall well-being.
supplementary_best: To remedy hot flashes, a combination of herbal essential oils can be effective. One of the best combinations is a blend of Vitex agnus-castus, Zingiber officinale, and Lavandula angustifolia. Vitex agnus-castus oil helps to regulate hormonal balance, which is often disrupted during hot flashes. It has been traditionally used to ease menopausal symptoms. Zingiber officinale oil, or ginger oil, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce heat and soothe the body. Lavandula angustifolia oil, or lavender oil, promotes relaxation and calms the nervous system, which can help to reduce hot flash frequency and severity. This combination is particularly effective because it addresses multiple aspects of hot flashes: hormonal balance, inflammation, and stress relief.
supplementary_avoid: If you experience hot flashes, there are some herbal essential oils you should use with caution. Clary sage oil (Salvia sclarea), for example, may worsen hot flashes due to its high estrogenic properties. This can stimulate the body's production of estrogen, potentially increasing the frequency and severity of hot flashes. Sage oil, on the other hand, can be beneficial for hot flashes, as it helps to reduce sweating and heat production. However, be aware that sage oil can be quite potent, and using it in large quantities or for extended periods may cause dizziness or other side effects. Another essential oil to approach with caution is Geranium oil (Pelargonium graveolens), which also has estrogenic properties. While it may help to alleviate hot flashes in some women, it can exacerbate the issue in others. As a result, it's recommended to use Geranium oil under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
supplementary_other_remedies: Some natural remedies that can help with hot flashes are flaxseed and vitamin E supplements. Flaxseed contains a compound called lignan, which has been shown to reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes in some women. The lignan in flaxseed may help block the production of estrogen, a hormone that can cause hot flashes. Vitamin E is an antioxidant that can help reduce inflammation in the body, which may contribute to hot flashes. Taking vitamin E supplements may help calm the nervous system and reduce the symptoms of hot flashes. Red clover is another natural remedy that has been used to help with hot flashes. It contains isoflavones, which are plant-based compounds that mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. Isoflavones in red clover may help reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes by blocking the production of estrogen. Additionally, some women have found relief from hot flashes by taking omega-3 fatty acid supplements, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and promote a healthy balance of hormones.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience hot flashes often have other health issues that can be treated with medicinal herbs. One common problem is night sweats. Black cohosh is a plant that can help reduce the frequency and severity of hot flashes and night sweats. It works by regulating hormones and reducing inflammation in the body.  Another issue associated with hot flashes is migraines. Feverfew, a herb rich in flavonoids, has been shown to reduce migraine frequency and severity. Its anti-inflammatory properties help block pain pathways in the brain.  Menopause-related hot flashes can also lead to osteoporosis. Dong quai, a Chinese herb, contains calcium and other minerals that help strengthen bones. It also regulates estrogen levels, which is essential for maintaining bone density.  Hot flashes can cause anxiety and insomnia in some people. Passionflower, a calming herb, helps reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. Its sedative properties also make it an effective treatment for insomnia.  Some people experience vaginal dryness and itching due to hormonal changes during menopause. Wild yam, a plant rich in estrogen-like compounds, can help alleviate these symptoms. Its anti-inflammatory properties also reduce vaginal irritation.  Ginger, a common herb, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce hot flash frequency and severity. It also regulates digestion and reduces nausea, which is often associated with menopause symptoms.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for hot flashes
